Definition: Unique identifier for a BOM link.
One parent/child link in the bill of materials.
Status: Draft, Released or Superseded.
The component consumed by the parent.
Date the BOM link took effect.
The assembly that consumes the child.
Unique identifier for a part record.
Descriptive name of the part.
Part number, format CPC-nnnnn; the identifier on every drawing, lot and certificate.
An item-master part: number, revision, type, cost and criticality.
Lifecycle status: Active, Obsolete or Hold.
Type: VALVE, FITTING, MANIFOLD, SEAL_KIT, ACTUATOR or RAW.
Quantity of the child consumed per unit of the parent.
Engineering revision letter; a shipped part must match its certificate revision.
TRUE for parts whose failure endangers people or equipment; drives full traceability and MRB authority.
Unit of measure: EA, KG or M.
Standard unit cost. Commercially sensitive - never customer-facing.
Approved-supplier-list status: Approved, Conditional or Suspended. A Suspended supplier must receive no new purchase orders.
City of the supplier.
Business contact email of the supplier.
Date the PO was issued.
Unique identifier for a purchase order.
Purchase order number, format PO-nnnnnnnn.
Status: Open, Received, Cancelled or Closed.
Part ordered.
Business phone number of the supplier.
Supplier-promised delivery date.
A purchase order to an ASL supplier.
Supplier quality rating: A, B or C.
Units ordered.
US state code of the supplier.
Date the current ASL status took effect.
Unique identifier for a supplier record.
Legal or trading name of the vendor.
An ASL vendor: status, rating and contacts.
Agreed unit price. Commercially sensitive.
Required completion date.
Part being produced.
Plant running the order.
Good units completed.
Units planned.
Units scrapped; feeds cost-of-quality reporting.
Planned or actual start date.
Unique identifier for a work order.
Work order number, format WO-nnnnnnnn; appears on travelers and lot records.
Status: Planned, Released, InProcess, Complete or Closed.
A production order with its quantities and status.
TRUE when a Certificate of Conformance is on file. DEFECT: two Released lots in this dataset carry no CoC - a lot must not be Released without one.
Unique identifier for a lot.
Lot number, format LOT-YYYY-nnnn; the traceability identifier on certificates, NCRs and shipments.
One made or purchased batch - the traceability spine.
Lifecycle: Quarantine, Released, Consumed or Recalled. Recalled lots trigger the customer-notification traceability query.
Date the lot was made or received.
Part the lot contains.
Units in the lot.
Source supplier (empty for made lots).
Producing work order (empty for purchased lots).
Defects found in the sample.
Date of the inspection.
Unique identifier for an inspection.
One inspection of one lot.
Type: INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, FINAL or AUDIT.
Inspector who performed it.
Lot inspected.
Outcome: PASS, FAIL or CONDITIONAL.
Units sampled.
Date the NCR was closed.
Defect: DIM_OOT, SURFACE, MATERIAL, PLATING or DOC.
Disposition: USE_AS_IS, REWORK, SCRAP or RTV.
Lot the nonconformance was found in.
TRUE when the Material Review Board signed the disposition. DEFECT: required for USE_AS_IS on MAJOR/CRITICAL - NCR-2026-014 lacks it.
Unique identifier for a nonconformance report.
NCR number, format NCR-YYYY-nnn.
Status: Open, Dispositioned or Closed.
A quality nonconformance and its disposition.
Employee who raised the NCR.
Date the NCR was opened.
Part affected.
Severity: MINOR, MAJOR or CRITICAL.
Customer legal name - business-contact data, commercially sensitive relationships.
Delivery city.
Delivery state.
Lot shipped - the outbound half of traceability; a recall follows this link to every affected customer.
Part shipped.
Units shipped.
Date the shipment left.
Unique identifier for a shipment.
Shipment number, format SH-nnnnnnnn.
A customer delivery by part and lot.
Status: Staged, Shipped, Delivered or Returned.
Email address of the person.
Unique identifier for an employee record.
A plant staff member: role, plant and status.
Employment status: Active or Inactive.
Given name of the person.
Date the employee was hired.
Family name of the person.
Employee who manages the plant.
Street address of the plant.
City the plant operates in.
Unique identifier for a plant record.
Name of a CPC manufacturing site.
Date the plant came on line.
Main phone number of the plant.
A CPC manufacturing site: location, manager and status.
US state of the plant.
Operating status: Active, Idle or Closed.
ZIP code of the plant address.
Job role: MACHINIST, QA_INSPECTOR, PLANT_MGR, BUYER, PLANNER or MFG_ENGINEER.
